title: RC Snubber Design for Transient Over-Voltage Mitigation in an RL Switching Circuit
description: Design and verify an RC snubber for transient over-voltage mitigation in a switched RL circuit. The generated circuit must be SPICE-simulatable, measurable through .meas directives, and evaluated under nominal and tolerance-aware conditions, while explicitly accounting for switching behavior, parasitic capacitance, and dissipated snubber energy.
objective: Obtain an RC snubber configuration that reduces switching-node over-voltage during turn-off transients while keeping the dissipated snubber energy within acceptable limits.

llm_task.expected_output: A valid ngspice-compatible netlist implementing a switched RL circuit with parasitic capacitance and an RC snubber, including transient analysis and .meas directives for peak voltage and dissipated snubber energy.
llm_task.topology_hint: Use a DC source feeding an RL load through an ideal switch, with a parasitic capacitance at the switching node and a series RC snubber connected across the switch or switching node according to a physically meaningful passive snubber topology.

measurements.name: vpeak_switch
measurements.description: Maximum voltage observed at the switching node during the turn-off transient window.

measurements.name: snubber_energy
measurements.description: Energy dissipated by the snubber during the transient observation window.

measurements.name: settling_proxy
measurements.description: Late-time residual maximum voltage used as a proxy for insufficient damping or persistent ringing.

all_of.rationale: The peak switching-node voltage must remain below the over-voltage limit.
The energy dissipated by the snubber must remain within an acceptable passive-loss budget.
Late-time residual over-voltage should remain limited, indicating acceptable damping of the transient.

robustness.description: Tolerance-aware robustness assessment under snubber component variations.
robustness.robust_acceptance_rule: All nominal acceptance criteria must remain satisfied for every sweep combination.

decision_protocol.run_definition: The generated netlist parses and executes successfully in ngspice, and all required transient measurements are extractable.
decision_protocol.pass_definition: The design satisfies all nominal acceptance criteria over the specified transient observation window.
decision_protocol.robust_pass_definition: The design satisfies all nominal acceptance criteria under all robustness sweep conditions.
decision_protocol.infeasibility_note: If multiple valid executable candidates consistently fail nominal acceptance despite physically meaningful topologies and measurable outputs, the framework may label the use case as specification-incompatible with the chosen circuit class.

checks: The switch model must be explicitly defined and connected consistently with the control signal.
The RL load must be explicitly present with Rload = 2 ohms and Lload = 10 mH.
The parasitic capacitance must be explicitly present at the switching node.
The snubber must be implemented as a passive RC network.
The transient window must include the turn-off event and the post-switching ringing interval.
Peak-voltage and energy measurements must use consistent node names and observation windows.

primary_selection_metric.name: vpeak_switch
primary_selection_metric.description: Among passing candidates, prefer the design with the lowest switching-node peak voltage.

secondary_selection_metrics.name: snubber_energy
secondary_selection_metrics.description: Prefer candidates that reduce the transient peak with lower dissipated energy.

expected_failure_modes: Transient simulation runs but no acceptable over-voltage reduction is achieved.
Snubber topology is physically meaningful but fails the nominal voltage limit.
Peak-voltage measurement is taken on the wrong node.
Energy expression is inconsistent with the actual snubber branch current or voltage.
The transient window misses the relevant turn-off event.
Nominal pass achieved but robustness failure under Rsnub or Csnub tolerance.
Specification is incompatible with the passive RC snubber circuit class under the imposed constraints.

metadata.paper_alignment: Reproducible SPICE-based verification of LLM-generated transient protection networks

snubber_energy.description: If snubber_energy measurement fails due to INTEG syntax issues, attempt to derive from average power or use alternative integration method.

vpeak_switch.description: Accept peak voltage measurement from any recognized switching node alias.
